rationale: Ground truth is dominated by reviewer asks for evidence (perf comparison, accuracy comparison, non-Qwen scope justification) and test coverage, most of which the author addressed via PR description data. X explicitly checks and cites that exact evidence (26.9%/-14.1% latency, SSIM/PSNR accuracy, FLUX non-Qwen validation), directly engaging the threads reviewers actually cared about, while Y's review is purely code/grep-grounded and never engages with the perf/accuracy justification at all. Both miss Gaohan123's 'pytest mark is not complete' comment. Both are well-grounded and actionable with concrete file:line follow-ups, but X covers substantially more of the real concerns.
